data.title: CA Cultural Technology Group Postpones AGM as Share Trading Remains Suspended
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An update from CA Cultural Technology Group Ltd. ( (HK:1566) ) is now available.

CA Cultural Technology Group Ltd. has postponed its annual general meeting, originally set for 29 April 2026, due to adjustments in its work schedule, and will fix a new date in due course. The associated book closure period for determining shareholder eligibility to attend and vote will also be rescheduled, with a fresh notice and meeting documents to be published and dispatched to shareholders.

Trading in the company’s shares on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange has been halted since 21 November 2024 at the company’s request and will remain suspended until further notice. The company plans to issue further announcements and quarterly updates under the Listing Rules to keep shareholders and potential investors informed, while advising them to exercise caution when dealing in its shares.

More about CA Cultural Technology Group Ltd.

CA Cultural Technology Group Ltd. is a Hong Kong-listed company incorporated in the Cayman Islands and traded on the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong under stock code 01566. The group operates in the cultural technology sector, though this announcement focuses on its corporate governance and listing status rather than specific products or services.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

i-CABLE Communications ( (HK:1097) ) has shared an update.

i-CABLE Communications has called its annual general meeting for 23 June 2026 in Hong Kong, where shareholders will receive the audited consolidated financial statements for the year ended 31 December 2025 and consider a slate of routine corporate governance matters. The agenda includes the re-election of several executive and independent non-executive directors, approval for the board to set directors’ and auditor’s remuneration, re-appointment of PricewaterhouseCoopers as auditor, and authorisations for the board to buy back up to 10% of the company’s shares and to issue additional shares, potentially enhancing capital management flexibility and influencing future shareholder returns.

The proposed share buy-back mandate would allow i-CABLE’s board to repurchase a limited portion of its issued share capital on the open market during the authorised period, while the general mandate to issue new shares or dispose of treasury shares would equip the company with tools to raise capital or optimise its balance sheet if needed. These resolutions, if approved, would align i-CABLE with common Hong Kong corporate practices, reinforcing board discretion over capital structure and signalling continued reliance on established external auditing and governance frameworks that may reassure investors about oversight and transparency.

More about i-CABLE Communications

i-CABLE Communications Limited is a Hong Kong-based communications and media company listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange. It operates in the telecommunications and broadcasting industry, providing cable TV, broadband and related services to consumers and businesses in Hong Kong, with a focus on subscription-based media and connectivity offerings in the local market.

data.description:

An announcement from FAR International Holdings Group Company Limited ( (HK:2516) ) is now available.

FAR International Holdings Group Company Limited has completed the first stage of a major acquisition, becoming the sole shareholder of COPE Holding and Hyperlining Holding after all conditions under the transaction agreement were satisfied. Through these holdings and a direct stake held by FAR Luxembourg Holdings Sarl, the group now controls 51% of COPE and 51% of Hyperlining, turning the target companies into subsidiaries whose financial results will be consolidated into the group’s accounts.

The move marks a significant expansion of FAR International’s consolidated asset base and operational footprint, as majority ownership of COPE and Hyperlining is expected to strengthen its control over these businesses and their contributions to group performance. The company signalled that further announcements will follow on its planned shareholding increase and final equity acquisition steps, indicating a phased strategy to deepen its ownership and potentially reshape its capital structure and earnings profile.

More about FAR International Holdings Group Company Limited

FAR International Holdings Group Company Limited is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong under stock code 2516. The group operates through multiple subsidiaries and uses holding structures, including FAR Luxembourg Holdings Sarl, to manage and consolidate its investments and equity interests in target businesses.

data.description:

  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Bank of Qingdao Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:3866) ) has issued an update.

Bank of Qingdao reported solid growth for the first quarter of 2026, with operating income rising 7.43% year-on-year to RMB 4.34 billion and net profit attributable to shareholders increasing 21.16% to RMB 1.52 billion. Basic and diluted earnings per share climbed to RMB 0.26, while annualized weighted average return on net assets improved to 14.63%, reflecting enhanced profitability.

The bank’s balance sheet also strengthened, as total assets expanded 2.36% from the end of 2025 to RMB 834.20 billion and equity attributable to shareholders grew 3.61% to RMB 50.94 billion, lifting net assets per share to RMB 7.31. Net cash flows from operating activities swung sharply into positive territory at RMB 12.78 billion, signaling improved liquidity and operational cash generation that should support ongoing growth and resilience for stakeholders.

More about Bank of Qingdao Co., Ltd. Class H

GCL New Energy Holdings has completed a share placement of 127 million new shares, representing about 7.55% of its enlarged issued share capital, at HK$1.05 per share to at least six independent investors. The latest announcement is out from Beijing UBOX Online Technology Corp. Class H ( (HK:2429) ).

Beijing UBOX Online Technology Corp. has renewed and expanded its cooperation with Alipay.com by signing a new Payment Services Framework Agreement and an Advertising Cooperation Framework Agreement, effective from January 1, 2026 to December 31, 2028. Under these arrangements, Alipay will continue to provide payment services enabling online transactions on UBOX’s vending machines, while both parties will collaborate on advertising and promoting Alipay’s payment products on the machines.

Because Alipay.com is an associate of a substantial shareholder of UBOX through Ant Group, the deals are classified as continuing connected transactions under Hong Kong listing rules. The size of the annual caps triggers reporting, announcement and annual review requirements, but the transactions are exempt from independent shareholders’ approval, streamlining compliance while formalizing a key strategic relationship in UBOX’s payments and advertising ecosystem.

More about Beijing UBOX Online Technology Corp. Class H

Beijing UBOX Online Technology Corp. operates smart vending machines and related online transaction infrastructure in China, providing automated retail services that rely heavily on digital payment channels. The company partners with major payment platforms to facilitate seamless cashless transactions for customers using its vending network across various locations.

An update from BYD Co ( (HK:1211) ) is now available.

BYD Company Limited has formalized detailed implementation rules for its Nomination Committee, a special committee under the board of directors tasked with overseeing the selection and governance of directors, the president and other senior officers. The rules define the committee’s composition, requiring at least three directors with a majority of independent non-executive directors, gender diversity, and an independent non-executive director as chairman, with terms aligned to those of the board.

Operational procedures are set out for convening meetings, voting, record-keeping and the use of teleconferencing and written resolutions, while ensuring adequate information and resources are provided to the committee. The Nomination Committee is mandated to annually review the board’s structure, size and skills mix, evaluate the independence of independent directors, and develop standards and procedures for senior appointments and succession planning, strengthening BYD’s corporate governance and board oversight as the company grows.

Hengan International Group Co ( (HK:1044) ) has provided an update.

Hengan International Group Company Limited has issued a clarification regarding its 2025 annual report, noting that the presentation unit for earnings per share on page 5 was misstated due to a clerical error. The company confirmed that the correct unit should be expressed in renminbi yuan rather than renminbi cents.

The board emphasized that this amendment is purely presentational and that all other information in the 2025 annual report remains unchanged, indicating no alteration to previously reported financial results. The prompt correction aims to maintain transparency and accuracy in shareholder communications, and suggests that the underlying financial position and disclosures of the group are unaffected by the error.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1044) stock is a Hold with a HK$26.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Hengan International Group Co stock, see the HK:1044 Stock Forecast page.

More about Hengan International Group Co

Hengan International Group Company Limited is a Hong Kong-listed company incorporated in the Cayman Islands and traded under stock code 1044. The group operates in the consumer goods sector, best known for manufacturing and selling personal hygiene and household paper products to mainland Chinese and broader Asian markets.

data.description:

Datang International Power Generation Co ( (HK:0991) ) has shared an update.

Datang International’s board met in Beijing on 28 April 2026, with all 15 directors present, and unanimously approved the company’s first-quarter 2026 report after prior review by its audit committee. The meeting was deemed legally compliant under PRC company law and the firm’s Articles of Association, underscoring stable governance and regulatory adherence.

The board also cleared the establishment of Datang (Ordos) Energy Development Co., Ltd., signaling an expansion of the company’s footprint in Ordos to support regional energy development. In a major capital move, directors approved investment of about RMB11.123 billion in a 4×660MW coal-fired power project backing the Kubuqi Desert to Jiangsu ultra-high voltage channel, a strategic base project that will bolster Datang International’s generation capacity and role in China’s large-scale energy transmission network.

More about Datang International Power Generation Co

Datang International Power Generation Co., Ltd. is a Sino-foreign joint stock company incorporated in the People’s Republic of China that focuses on power generation and related energy projects. Listed in Hong Kong, the company operates large-scale coal-fired power assets and participates in major energy base developments linked to ultra-high voltage transmission channels in China.
